design_philosophy.core_loop: Select team, play ice hockey matches (exhibition or playoff tournament), score goals while defending against opponent AI
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time positional play and timing in hockey simulation; balancing offensive pressure with defensive positioning
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Manage and play as a professional NHL hockey team with authentic rosters and realistic match mechanics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commitment to authenticity: uses real player names and 1991-92 season rosters despite NHLPA-only licensing; includes realistic mechanics like injuries and glass shattering; prioritizes strategic depth and simulation accuracy over arcade accessibility

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Matches can be replayed; playoff tournament structure allows progression through competitive play; reviewers noted AI was not sufficiently challenging, suggesting goals were 'too easy' to score

progression.unlock_mechanism: Tournament advancement through playoff mode (single-elimination or best-of-7 formats); players advance by winning matches to collect Stanley Cup-style trophy
replayability_hooks: All 24 authentic NHL teams from 1991-92 season selectable
Playoff tournament with variable series length
Save/password system (SNES uses passwords; Genesis uses EEPROM to save lines and ongoing playoff progress)

world_building_techniques: Authentic team rosters and player names
Realistic simulation of NHL hockey mechanics
Stanley Cup trophy as tournament reward

faction_indicators: 24 distinct NHL teams from 1991-92 season (with city-based naming due to NHLPA licensing only)
environmental_storytelling: Real player rosters and team composition
Injury system reflecting realistic gameplay consequences
